description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone with a classic design, featuring a black and gold color scheme. The base is ornate, with metallic gold accents and a circular decorative plate at the front, supported by four curved legs. The handset rests horizontally on two cradles, with a coiled brown cord connecting it to the main body. The earpiece and mouthpiece are both cylindrical and finished in black and gold, while the main body of the phone is a rectangular black box with metallic details. There is a rotary dial on the front, and a side lever is visible, likely for ringing or switching lines. The overall appearance is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones, often found in private homes or offices.
description: A vintage black and gold corded telephone with a rotary dial, ornate base, and a coiled cord connecting the handset to the main body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, ornate gold plate, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the circular side lever and the profile of the handset and cradle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset, the coiled cord, and the structural supports of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the coiled cord attachment and the metallic lever on the side.
